title: Why Shares of Truist, KeyCorp, and Comerica Are Falling Today
text: In the first quarter, the U.S. banking system saw its largest decline in deposits in almost four decades. The FDIC's bank "problem list" also grew in the first quarter.

title: Bank Stocks Tumble As FDIC Warns Of 'Downside Risks.' Full Fallout From March Panic Unclear.
text: Bank stocks fell Wednesday after the FDIC chairman said in a quarterly report that "downside risks" remain for the banking industry.

title: KeyCorp: Dividend Is Interesting But Consider These Risks
text: KeyCorp's 8% dividend yield is interesting, but fundamentals are struggling. The bank's financial situation is stable, but factors such as a declining net interest margin, rising deposit beta, and the Fed's monetary policy could impact earnings and dividend growth. Investors should focus on banks with solid fundamentals rather than those with the highest dividend yield.

title: Why I Sold KeyCorp's 7.4% Yielding Series E Preferred Shares
text: Recently, I reviewed KeyCorp's first quarter financials to check on my long position in the company's Series E Preferred shares. The bank has a large balance of uninsured deposits, high leverage, and a thin proportion of cash on hand to deposits. Based on my review of the bank's financials, I opted to sell my holdings.

title: Bank Panic Creates Old-Fashioned Arbitrage Opportunities, 13% From KeyCorp Preferred Stocks
text: 2 similar products trade on different markets at different valuations. Recent bank volatility is responsible for some sharp moves that create mispricings. 13% potential mispricing is a rare find in the fixed income world.

title: KeyCorp's Stock Has Now Fallen More Than the Broader Banking Sector. Is Now the Time to Buy?
text: The bank has experienced pressure on its margins. KeyCorp also has a low tangible common-equity ratio.

title: New York City Banking Commission votes to freeze Capital One, KeyBank deposits
text: The New York City Banking Commission voted to freeze the city's deposits at Capital One and KeyBank after the banks failed to submit plans on their efforts to root out discrimination.

title: NYC banking commission votes to halt city deposits at Capital One, KeyBank
text: New York City's banking commission voted to freeze deposits at Capital One Financial Corp. COF, +0.43% and KeyCorp's KEY, -0.88% KeyBank after the two banks failed to submit required plans showing their efforts to halt discrimination, the city's comptroller said in a statement on Thursday, alleging that the banks “outright refused to submit required policies.” The comptroller, Brad Lander, also voted against designating Wells Fargo & Co. WFC, +0.07%, PNC Financial Services Group Inc.'s PNC, -1.05% PNC Bank and International Finance Bank to hold public funds, that statement said.

title: Bank Of America Upped Its Forecast For Stocks — Just Not The Ones You Think
text: Bank of America is getting more bullish on S&P 500 stocks. Just not the ones that have been driving the S&P 500 higher all year.

title: Western Alliance, KeyCorp Fall as Report Says Yellen Notes More Bank Mergers May Be Needed
text: Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen told bank chief executives that more bank mergers may be necessary, according to a report from CNN.

title: Why Shares of Capital One, PacWest Bancorp, and KeyCorp Rallied This Week
text: Warren Buffett's company purchased shares of Capital One in the first quarter. Scion Asset Management also purchased shares of Capital One and PacWest Bancorp in the first quarter.

title: Why Shares of Western Alliance, KeyCorp, and Charles Schwab Rose Today
text: A Wall Street analyst upgraded Charles Schwab. Famous investor Michael Burry bought shares of several beaten-down bank stocks in the first quarter.

title: U.S. regional bank shares look to recover from bruising selloff
text: Shares of major U.S. regional banks booked slim gains in premarket trading on Friday, a day after getting hammered by deposit declines at PacWest and a banking regulator's decision to impose a fee on uninsured deposits.

title: KeyCorp (KEY) Hurt by Rising Expenses & Loan Concentration
text: A lack of loan diversification and a steady rise in operating expenses are major near-term headwinds for KeyCorp (KEY).

title: KRE ETF surges as PacWest, Comerica, KeyCorp stocks lead
text: Regional bank stocks continued surging on Monday as the recent concerns about the industry faded. The SPDR S&P Regional Banking ETF (KRE) stock jumped by more than 2% in the pre-market after it rose by over 6% on Friday.

title: Want To Buy A Beaten Down Bank Stock? Here's A List And Analysis
text: Thirteen badly beaten-up bank stocks are reviewed and compared to those that were recently taken over. Items reviewed include uninsured deposits, risky activities, unrealized investment losses, capital ratios, ROE, performance in the last recession, and loan growth.

title: KEY FAMILY WEALTH RECOGNIZED AS A LEADER IN 'WEALTH COUNSELLING' AT THE 2023 FAMILY WEALTH REPORT AWARDS
text: CLEVELAND , May 5, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Key Family Wealth, the multi-family office division of KeyBank, was named "Best Family Wealth Counselling" for its enhanced consultative services at the 2023 Family Wealth Report (FWR) awards. The annual FWR awards recognizes the most innovative and exceptional firms, teams and individuals serving the family office, family wealth and trusted advisor communities in North America.

title: Is It Time to Buy the S&P 500's 4 Worst-Performing Stocks of 2023?
text: Regional banks are reeling in the wake of several significant failures. And there's no clear indication the crisis is over.

title: Why Shares of Western Alliance Bancorp, U.S. Bancorp, and KeyCorp Are Falling Today
text: Rumors were circulating earlier that Western Alliance was exploring a sale, but the bank has since denied these reports. Regional banks have had a difficult week, despite reporting earnings not long ago that seemed to reassure the market.

title: KEYCORP RAISES ITS PRIME LENDING RATE TO 8.25 PERCENT
text: CLEVELAND , May 3, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- KeyCorp (NYSE: KEY) and its banking affiliates have raised their prime lending rate to 8.25 percent from 8.00 percent, effective tomorrow, May 4, 2023. About KeyCorp KeyCorp's roots trace back nearly 200 years to Albany, New York.

title: Regional Banks Continue to Bleed as Another Rate Hike Looms
text: The Fed is likely to announce another 25-basis point hike in rates. The U.S. regional banks like PACW, WAL, ZION, CMA and KEY witness bearish investor sentiments on concerns over the impact of future rate hikes on financials.

title: First Republic Bank sale fails to quash regional banking fears
text: Shares of regional US banks fell on Tuesday with investors seemingly not convinced that the sale of First Republic Bank (NYSE:FRC)'s assets to JPMorgan over the weekend is enough to stabilize the troubled sector.  JPMorgan agreed to purchase First Republic's deposits in a bid to restore stability to the ailing sector after alternative negotiations for a rescue package failed.

title: Why KeyCorp Stock Dropped 10% in April
text: KeyCorp, the holding company for KeyBank, saw net income decline year over year in the first quarter. Several analysts lowered their price targets for the bank after the earnings release.

title: Why Shares of PacWest Bancorp, KeyCorp, and Comerica Are Falling Today
text: The seizure of First Republic will add to losses in the FDIC Deposit Insurance Fund. Investors are reassessing their approach when it comes to bank stocks.

title: S&P 500: Nvidia Stock Is A Top Performer, While Regional Banks Skid
text: On Semiconductor and Nvidia were among S&P 500 winners Monday, while regional bank stocks were among the worst performers. The post S&P 500: Nvidia Stock Is A Top Performer, While Regional Banks Skid appeared first on Investor's Business Daily.
